domain: predicting the boundary of clean concept-detachment in language models

backlog.name: Separability-failure predictor (forecast from internals WHERE clean concept-detachment holds vs breaks, and BEAT the orthogonality baseline)
backlog.definition: Reuses the working steering harness (research_cycle/experiments/category_slot/) on gpt2 / pythia-160m (CPU). Build MANY configs (entity E, swap category C->C', preserved attribute A) across domains (e.g. apple:fruit->vegetable keep red/sweet/round; tomato:vegetable<->fruit; whale:fish->mammal keep big/swims; bat:bird->mammal keep flies; ...), aim >=30-40 (E,C->C',A) triples. For each, EMPIRICALLY map clean-detachment: sweep steering strength alpha; record FLIP(alpha)=category moved to C', PRESERVE_A(alpha)=attribute A survived, COHERENCE(alpha)=perplexity in band. Derive per-config OUTCOME scalars: clean-window width = range of alpha with FLIP>=0.7 AND PRESERVE>=0.7 AND coherent; break-alpha = strength where it collapses. GOAL: predict these outcomes from the model's INTERNALS computed WITHOUT the full sweep. Candidate predictors: (BASELINE = Park's theory) cosine/angle between dir(C->C') and dir(A) under the causal inner product — orthogonal => separable; (OURS to test) causal-read overlap = do the SAME downstream components (heads/MLPs) read from both directions (attribution/gradient overlap), and superposition/probe-interference of A near the C axis. Fit predictors on a TRAIN split of configs, test on a DISJOINT split.
backlog.prediction: On held-out configs, the best INTERNAL predictor forecasts the empirical clean-window (or break-alpha) with Spearman >= 0.5 AND beats the pure-orthogonality (Park) baseline by a preregistered margin (delta-Spearman >= 0.15, bootstrap 95% CI excludes 0). FALSIFIED-as-rediscovery if NO predictor beats the orthogonality baseline (then we add nothing over Park). FALSIFIED-null if nothing beats a random predictor (internals carry no boundary signal). Oracle: the steering positive control (add +C' to a real C'-member intensifies C') must pass, else BROKEN_MEASUREMENT.
backlog.reduces_to: PRIOR ART IS STIPULATED — historian: treat these as GIVEN, do NOT re-derive or flag them, the experiment already concedes them: Park et al. 2024 'Geometry of Categorical and Hierarchical Concepts' (categorical simplices, hierarchy orthogonality, orthogonal => independent intervention) [arXiv:2406.01506]; the Linear Representation Hypothesis + 'causal separability' (Park/Choe/Veitch); RAVEL benchmark for entity-attribute disentanglement [arXiv:2402.17700]; ActAdd/activation steering [arXiv:2308.10248]; 'Understanding Unreliability of Steering Vectors' [arXiv:2602.17881]. The bare claims 'categories are separable coordinates' and 'orthogonality => clean separation' are GIVEN. This concept EARNS KEEP ONLY IF it does BOTH: (1) characterizes the FAILURE BOUNDARY — where/at what strength clean detachment breaks — which the success-oriented literature and RAVEL's aggregate scores do NOT map; AND (2) provides an a-priori internal predictor of that boundary that BEATS the orthogonality baseline. If it merely re-confirms orthogonality=>separability, that IS the refuted-as-rediscovery outcome, report it as such.

seenStress: PRIOR ART GIVEN (do not re-mine): Park et al. hierarchy geometry [2406.01506], LRH + causal separability, RAVEL disentanglement benchmark [2402.17700], ActAdd steering [2308.10248]. OPEN GAP we target: the literature idealizes or aggregate-scores separability; the FAILURE BOUNDARY (which concept pairs break, at what strength) and an a-priori INTERNAL predictor of it that beats the orthogonality baseline are not established.
